posts.name: Federica Riti
posts.text:
Dear Experts,
I am trying to implement a fit which includes a signal (S) and two backgrounds: X (from MC simulation) and Y (data-driven).
The normalisation of bkg X is left floating in the fit as RateParam x.
The other background Y is data driven and its shape changes depending on the value of x, in a non trivial way.
For Y I was thinking of using the morphing function RooMorphingPdf to interpolate between some shapes obtained at different x. Do you think it is the right approach? I saw the definition here [0], but I don’t think I can use the BuildRooMorphing function here described, because of how my workspace is already defined. Can I directly define a RooMorphingPdf in my workspace?
Also I am not sure how to communicate to Combine in the workspace (or in the datacard?) that the variable associated to the morphing is the RateParam x already defined for the bkg X normalisation.
Thanks a lot in advance,
Federica Riti
[0] https://cms-analysis.github.io/CombineHarvester/intro_morph.html
posts.cooked_html:
Dear Experts,

I am trying to implement a fit which includes a signal (S) and two backgrounds: X (from MC simulation) and Y (data-driven).
The normalisation of bkg X is left floating in the fit as RateParam x.
The other background Y is data driven and its shape changes depending on the value of x, in a non trivial way.

For Y I was thinking of using the morphing function RooMorphingPdf to interpolate between some shapes obtained at different x. Do you think it is the right approach? I saw the definition here [0], but I don’t think I can use the BuildRooMorphing function here described, because of how my workspace is already defined. Can I directly define a RooMorphingPdf in my workspace?

Also I am not sure how to communicate to Combine in the workspace (or in the datacard?) that the variable associated to the morphing is the RateParam x already defined for the bkg X normalisation.

Thanks a lot in advance,
Federica Riti

[0] https://cms-analysis.github.io/CombineHarvester/intro_morph.html
